ai_analysis.one_sentence_summary: frontend is user-exposed and still automounts the default SA token—strip it while you harden ingress because a compromised web pod shouldn’t get a free Kubernetes credential.
ai_analysis.true_positive_rationale: Classic default-SA token case on an Internet-adjacent workload; ACS is reporting the exact condition the policy describes.
ai_analysis.attack_vector: Web RCE plus token theft could bootstrap cluster reconnaissance from a public entrypoint.
ai_analysis.blast_radius: Entire cluster perception if default SA RBAC or routes allow lateral movement beyond ms-demo.
ai_analysis.prioritization_rationale: MEDIUM baseline with an exposure bump for being the browser-facing tier in the demo topology.
recommended_actions: Set automountServiceAccountToken false on the frontend Deployment unless server.go truly needs API access.
Review Route and NetworkPolicy to ensure only required ports are reachable.
Combine with WAF/rate limits while remediating underlying mount hygiene.
mitigation_strategies: Canary the change: watch error budgets after deploy since some demos wire unexpected readiness hooks.
